QF.id: Duplicate Share Certificates and Transmission of Securities_16

question.text: From January 1, 2022, which of the following must be furnished by physical holders before any service request can be processed?

answer.text: PAN, KYC, and Nomination (or opt-out) documents

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** From January 1, 2022, SEBI mandated that no service requests shall be processed for physical holders unless three mandatory requirements are fulfilled: PAN card, KYC completion, and Nomination registration (or formal opt-out via Form ISR-3). All three are prerequisites.
**The Trap:** Having just PAN or just PAN + KYC is insufficient. The nomination requirement (or explicit opt-out) is a relatively new addition that many candidates overlook. Aadhaar alone is not sufficient — PAN is specifically mandated.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The common KYC form for physical holders is Form ISR-1. Nomination is registered via Form SH-13 and opt-out via Form ISR-3. These forms work together to ensure complete investor identification and succession planning.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When onboarding physical shareholders or processing any service request, use a checklist: PAN (verified), KYC (Form ISR-1 filed), Nomination (Form SH-13 filed OR Form ISR-3 opt-out). Missing any one will block all service requests.
